description: You are a data-analyst who has just been asked to do a quick security scan of some raw CSV files that arrived in /home/user/data/. 1. Inspect every *.csv file found directly under /home/user/data/ (there are exactly two of them). 2. For each file, count: • total_rows – the number of data rows (DO NOT count the header row) • email_hits – how many e-mail addresses you can see (simple rule: any field that contains an “@” counts) • cc_hits – how many payment-card numbers you can see (simple rule: a token of 13-19 digits that may optionally be separated every 4 digits by a dash “-” counts) 3. Create a directory called /home/user/security_scan/ (if it does not already exist) and inside it create the text file scan_report.log summarising your findings. • One CSV file per line. • Use exactly the following comma-separated column order (no spaces): filename,total_rows,email_hits,cc_hits,scan_timestamp • filename must contain ONLY the basename (for example sales_q1.csv). • scan_timestamp must be an ISO-8601 value in the form YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ. • Finish the file with a single trailing newline (no extra blank lines). Example of the required formatting (values will differ): sales_q1.csv,3,3,0,2027-01-30T15:22:04Z 4. Produce sanitised copies of every scanned CSV so that no PII remains: • Put the copies in /home/user/data_sanitized/ using the same filenames. • Replace every e-mail address and every payment-card number with the literal text [REDACTED] (including the square brackets). • Keep all other data and the headers unchanged and preserve the original column ordering. 5. Verify your own work on the console by showing (a) the contents of scan_report.log and (b) the first 10 lines of each sanitised CSV. Hints • Everything can be done with standard command-line tools such as grep, awk, sed, date, etc. • The entire job can easily be done in 5-10 commands.
truth: Initial environment before the task starts: 1. The directory /home/user/data/ already exists and contains exactly two CSV files. 1.1 /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv Contents (4 lines): -------------------------------------------------- order_id,customer_email,amount 1001,alice@example.com,250.00 1002,bob@gmail.com,175.50 1003,charlie@example.com,300.00 -------------------------------------------------- 1.2 /home/user/data/customer_info.csv Contents (4 lines): -------------------------------------------------- customer_id,name,email,credit_card c001,Alice Smith,alice@example.com,4111-1111-1111-1111 c002,Bob Johnson,bob@gmail.com,5500-0000-0000-0004 c003,Charlie Brown,charlie@example.com,6011-0009-9013-9424 -------------------------------------------------- 2. No other files or directories mentioned in the public description exist yet. • /home/user/security_scan/ does NOT exist. • /home/user/data_sanitized/ does NOT exist. Expected final state AFTER the agent finishes: A. Directory structure • /home/user/security_scan/scan_report.log (file) • /home/user/data_sanitized/ (directory) ├─ sales_q1.csv └─ customer_info.csv B. /home/user/security_scan/scan_report.log must contain exactly two non-empty lines and finish with a single newline character. Line 1 (sales_q1.csv) MUST have the following first four comma-separated fields in order: sales_q1.csv,3,3,0 Line 2 (customer_info.csv) MUST have the following first four comma-separated fields in order: customer_info.csv,3,3,3 The fifth field on each line (scan_timestamp) is free-form ISO-8601 and will NOT be checked by the tests; any valid UTC timestamp is acceptable. Example of valid final file (timestamps illustrate formatting only): sales_q1.csv,3,3,0,2024-02-05T12:00:22Z customer_info.csv,3,3,3,2024-02-05T12:00:22Z C. Sanitised CSVs (exact contents expected): C.1 /home/user/data_sanitized/sales_q1.csv order_id,customer_email,amount 1001,[REDACTED],250.00 1002,[REDACTED],175.50 1003,[REDACTED],300.00 C.2 /home/user/data_sanitized/customer_info.csv customer_id,name,email,credit_card c001,Alice Smith,[REDACTED],[REDACTED] c002,Bob Johnson,[REDACTED],[REDACTED] c003,Charlie Brown,[REDACTED],[REDACTED] Notes for the automated tester • Ignore the exact value of the fifth column (scan_timestamp) but ensure it matches the regex [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z. • Verify that the scan_report.log file has exactly two lines plus a final newline—no leading or trailing blank lines. • Verify that every “[REDACTED]” token is uppercase and includes the square brackets. • Ensure no unsanitised e-mails or credit-card numbers remain anywhere in /home/user/data_sanitized/.
name: task_000000_a47279aa
